name: Laparoscopic splenectomy
sources: Model-generated (textbook-level); verify against an operative atlas splenectomy chapter and SAGES guidance.
review_note: Splenic hilum-to-pancreatic-tail relationship and exact step order must be checked against your atlas; accessory spleen handling depends on indication.

intake.why: Determines composition and how much can be shown clearly
One figure cannot show everything; focus drives which structures dominate
A hematologic indication (e.g. ITP) requires showing/searching for accessory spleens

figure_scopes.overview: Spleen with its ligaments and vascular supply in the left upper quadrant
figure_scopes.storyboard: Multi-panel: splenocolic mobilization -> gastrosplenic ligament and short gastrics -> hilum exposure -> hilar control protecting the pancreatic tail -> retrieval
figure_scopes.closeup: Single splenic hilum panel showing the safe plane from the pancreatic tail

laterality: Left upper quadrant, subdiaphragmatic, behind ribs 9-11; splenic vein lies posterior to the pancreas, splenic artery along the superior border of the pancreas
must_have: Splenorenal, gastrosplenic, splenocolic, and splenophrenic ligaments.
spatial_rules: The splenic artery runs along the superior border of the pancreas and is tortuous; the splenic vein lies posterior to the pancreas.
Short gastric vessels travel within the gastrosplenic ligament.
The tail of the pancreas lies close to the splenic hilum and reaches it in roughly half of people.

forbidden_errors.error: Hilar vessel division shown without a safe plane from the pancreatic tail (implying tail injury).
Spleen drawn in the right upper quadrant (wrong laterality).
Accessory spleen omitted for a hematologic indication.

safety_concept: Protect the tail of the pancreas during hilar control to avoid pancreatic leak/fistula.
phases: Mobilize the splenic flexure / divide the splenocolic ligament.
Divide the gastrosplenic ligament and short gastric vessels.
Expose the splenic hilum via the splenorenal ligament.
Control the splenic artery and vein, staying clear of the pancreatic tail.
verify: Spleen in the left upper quadrant, subdiaphragmatic, behind the ribs.
Safe plane shown between the hilar vessels and the pancreatic tail; tail uninjured. [model-generated - check carefully against your atlas]
Short gastric vessels within the gastrosplenic ligament.
Splenic artery along the superior pancreatic border; splenic vein posterior to the pancreas.
For a hematologic indication, accessory spleen labeled (hilum / pancreatic tail / ligaments / omentum).
